scenario_results.reason: Topology guidance stays inside commerce-platform configuration, sequences channel and market dependencies clearly, and avoids invented tenant assumptions.
Catalog recommendations are tied to vendor platform behavior, validation steps, and rollback handling rather than generic merchandising advice.
Checkout guidance remains configuration-scoped, states escalation boundaries, and avoids unsupported legal or payment claims.
Integration planning remains platform-scoped, covers connector ownership and rate-limit constraints, and rejects implementation work.
Release-readiness output uses vendor changelogs and release notes to define safe update sequencing, test gates, and rollback checkpoints.
Operations triage guidance prioritizes monitoring, order-flow evidence, and escalation boundaries for commerce incidents.
Role and governance handling stays within least-privilege and approval guidance and requires human signoff for policy-sensitive decisions.
Unsupported extension or deprecated interface usage is flagged clearly, supported alternatives are preferred, and uncertainty is escalated instead of guessed through.
Custom storefront engineering work is rejected explicitly while preserving in-scope commerce platform review guidance.
Marketing strategy ownership is separated cleanly from platform scope and routed back to the orchestrator.
Missing tenant evidence triggers low-confidence escalation and a required-artifacts checklist instead of unsafe production recommendations.
Shared meta-agent outages fall back safely to the reviewed local baseline with freshness limits called out.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against issue #144 requirements, covering normal commerce-platform configuration and administration, integration and workflow quality, operations and governance handling, edge constraints, out-of-scope rejection, low-confidence escalation, and shared-meta-agent fallback.
No unreviewed regressions remain and human verification was recorded in the readiness evidence.
